--- title: "Multiple Imputation" format: live-html: css: - exams/webex.css include-after-body: exams/webex.js webr: packages: - dplyr - haven - naniar - mice - mitml - miceadds toc: true lang: de embed-resources: true --- ::: {.cell} ::: # Überblick Die multiple Imputation (MI) ist eine sehr mächtige Methode zum Umgang mit fehlenden Werten, die auf der Idee basiert, mehrere plausible Werte für die fehlenden Daten zu generieren. Diese Methode wurde von Rubin [-@rubin1976] entwickelt und hat sich seitdem als eine der Standardmethoden für den Umgang mit fehlenden Daten etabliert. MI besteht aus drei Hauptschritten (siehe @fig-mi-workflow): 1) **Imputation**: In diesem Schritt werden mehrere vollständige Datensätze generiert, indem fehlende Werte durch plausible Werte ersetzt werden. Dies geschieht in der Regel durch die Verwendung von Modellen, die auf den beobachteten Daten basieren, um die fehlenden Werte zu schätzen. Es können verschiedene Imputationsmodelle verwendet werden, z.B. lineare Regression, logistische Regression, ordinale Regression, predictive mean matching oder Random Forests. 2) **Analyse**: Jeder der generierten vollständigen Datensätze wird einzeln analysiert, z.B. durch die Schätzung von Regressionsmodellen, Effektstärken, Hypothesentests. 3) **Pooling**: Die Ergebnisse der Analysen der einzelnen Datensätze werden anschließend zusammengeführt, um eine einzige Schätzung und Inferenzstatistik zu erhalten. Hierzu werden spezielle Pooling-Methoden verwendet, die die Unsicherheit berücksichtigen, die durch die Imputation der fehlenden Werte entsteht. ![Workflow des Missing Data Handling mit multipler Imputation](img/mi-workflow.svg){#fig-mi-workflow style="width: 90%"} Im Umfeld des `{mice}`-Packages in R ist zu diesem Workflow auch die folgende Terminologie gebräuchlich geworden: | Klasse in `{mice}` | Häufiges Objekt Suffix | `{mice}`-Funktion | Beschreibung | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | mids | imp | mice() | Multipel imputierter Datensatz | | mild | idl | complete() | Liste multipel imputierter Daten | | mira | fit | with() | Wiederholte Analysen mit multipler Imputation | | mipo | est | pool() | Pooling der Ergebnisse aus multipler Imputation | : Terminologie im Umfeld von `{mice}` (siehe @vanbuuren2018) # Multiple Imputation in `{mice}` Im oben beschriebenen Workflow ist die multiple Imputation besonders herausfordernd. Sie muss auf die diversen Arten (Skalenniveau, Kontinuität) und Verteilungen von Daten angepasst werden, die Missingness Patterns berücksichtigen und jeweils **alle** Prädiktoren der Missingness enthalten. Diese Schritte werden im folgenden Beispiel Schritt für Schritt bearbeitet # Ein artifizielles `{mice}`-Beispiel ## Datengrundlage ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.2.0 ✔ readr 2.1.6 ✔ forcats 1.0.1 ✔ stringr 1.6.0 ✔ ggplot2 4.0.1 ✔ tibble 3.3.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.4 ✔ tidyr 1.3.2 ✔ purrr 1.2.1 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package () to force all conflicts to become errors ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} N <- 1000 data_art <- tibble( x_comp = rnorm(N), y_comp = 2 + 0.5 * x_comp + rnorm(N), r_y = rbinom(N, 1, prob = plogis(.2 + 3 * x_comp)), r_x = rbinom(N, 1, prob = plogis(4 - 3 * y_comp)), y_beob = ifelse(r_y == 0, y_comp, NA), x_beob = x_comp ) ``` ::: ## Missingness Pattern ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(naniar) vis_miss(data_art, sort_miss = TRUE, cluster = TRUE) + theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 20, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## Missingness Mechanism ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(data_art, aes(x = x_beob, y = y_beob)) + geom_miss_point(jitter = 0.1) + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## Imputation ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(mice) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Attaching package: 'mice' ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` The following object is masked from 'package:stats': filter ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` The following objects are masked from 'package:base': cbind, rbind ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} data_art_imp <- mice( data_art %>% select(x_beob, y_beob), m = 5, seed = 1895 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` iter imp variable 1 1 y_beob 1 2 y_beob 1 3 y_beob 1 4 y_beob 1 5 y_beob 2 1 y_beob 2 2 y_beob 2 3 y_beob 2 4 y_beob 2 5 y_beob 3 1 y_beob 3 2 y_beob 3 3 y_beob 3 4 y_beob 3 5 y_beob 4 1 y_beob 4 2 y_beob 4 3 y_beob 4 4 y_beob 4 5 y_beob 5 1 y_beob 5 2 y_beob 5 3 y_beob 5 4 y_beob 5 5 y_beob ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} summary(data_art_imp) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Class: mids Number of multiple imputations: 5 Imputation methods: x_beob y_beob "" "pmm" PredictorMatrix: x_beob y_beob x_beob 0 1 y_beob 1 0 ``` ::: ::: ## Visual Check des Imputationsalgorithmus ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(data_art_imp) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## Visual Check der Imputation ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggmice) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Attaching package: 'ggmice' ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` The following objects are masked from 'package:mice': bwplot, densityplot, stripplot, xyplot ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} plot_flux(data_art %>% select(x_beob, y_beob)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=2100} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} mice::xyplot(data_art_imp, y_beob ~ x_beob) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-2.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## Vergleich Regressionsanalytischer Ergebnisse ### Gepoolte Imputierte Regression ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} reg_fit <- with(data_art_imp, lm(scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob))) summary(reg_fit) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 10 × 7 term estimate std.error statistic p.value nobs df.residual 1 (Intercept) 2.33e-15 0.0283 8.24e-14 1.000e+ 0 1000 998 2 scale(x_beob) 4.47e- 1 0.0283 1.58e+ 1 2.10 e-50 1000 998 3 (Intercept) 2.75e-15 0.0289 9.50e-14 1.000e+ 0 1000 998 4 scale(x_beob) 4.05e- 1 0.0289 1.40e+ 1 1.11 e-40 1000 998 5 (Intercept) 4.33e-15 0.0283 1.53e-13 1.000e+ 0 1000 998 6 scale(x_beob) 4.48e- 1 0.0283 1.58e+ 1 1.45 e-50 1000 998 7 (Intercept) 2.19e-15 0.0280 7.83e-14 1.000e+ 0 1000 998 8 scale(x_beob) 4.65e- 1 0.0280 1.66e+ 1 6.86 e-55 1000 998 9 (Intercept) 3.98e-15 0.0289 1.38e-13 1.000e+ 0 1000 998 10 scale(x_beob) 4.10e- 1 0.0289 1.42e+ 1 7.38 e-42 1000 998 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} reg_fit ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` call : with.mids(data = data_art_imp, expr = lm(scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob))) call1 : mice(data = data_art %>% select(x_beob, y_beob), m = 5, seed = 1895) nmis : [1] 0 513 analyses : [[1]] Call: lm(formula = scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob)) Coefficients: (Intercept) scale(x_beob) 2.332e-15 4.475e-01 [[2]] Call: lm(formula = scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob)) Coefficients: (Intercept) scale(x_beob) 2.748e-15 4.046e-01 [[3]] Call: lm(formula = scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob)) Coefficients: (Intercept) scale(x_beob) 4.325e-15 4.482e-01 [[4]] Call: lm(formula = scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob)) Coefficients: (Intercept) scale(x_beob) 2.192e-15 4.654e-01 [[5]] Call: lm(formula = scale(y_beob) ~ scale(x_beob)) Coefficients: (Intercept) scale(x_beob) 3.976e-15 4.102e-01 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} reg_est <- pool(reg_fit) summary(reg_est) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` term estimate std.error statistic df p.value 1 (Intercept) 3.114749e-15 0.02847602 1.093815e-13 996.00599 1.000000e+00 2 scale(x_beob) 4.351664e-01 0.04061074 1.071555e+01 15.03445 1.952724e-08 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} reg_est ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Class: mipo m = 5 term m estimate ubar b t dfcom 1 (Intercept) 5 3.114749e-15 0.0008108835 9.517112e-31 0.0008108835 998 2 scale(x_beob) 5 4.351664e-01 0.0008116952 6.979472e-04 0.0016492318 998 df riv lambda fmi 1 996.00599 1.408406e-27 1.408406e-27 0.00200199 2 15.03445 1.031836e+00 5.078344e-01 0.56241501 ``` ::: ::: ### Listwise Deletion und vollständige Daten ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} reg_listwise <- lm(y_beob ~ x_beob, data = data_art) reg_comp <- lm(y_comp ~ x_comp, data = data_art) sjPlot::tab_model(reg_listwise, reg_comp, show.std = T, show.ci = F) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
  y beob y comp
Predictors Estimates std. Beta p Estimates std. Beta p
(Intercept) 2.03 -0.00 <0.001 2.02 -0.00 <0.001
x beob 0.48 0.31 <0.001
x comp 0.51 0.47 <0.001
Observations 487 1000
R2 / R2 adjusted 0.093 / 0.092 0.220 / 0.219
````` ::: ::: # Ein »Real World« `{mice}`-Beispiel ## Datengrundlage Als Datengrundlage verwenden wir einen Ausschnitt des STAR-Experiments, indem die experimentell die Wirkung einer Klassengrößenreduktion auf die Schulleistung untersucht wurde [@word1990]. Um zunächst das Problem genesteter Daten zu umgehen, verwenden wir nur die Daten je einer Schüler:in pro Klasse. Die Daten enthalten die folgenden Variablen: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(haven) library(dplyr) library(codebook) set.seed(1895) data_star <- read_sav( "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sammerk/GRAPH-Missing-Data/master/data_missing_workshop.sav" ) %>% group_by(g3tchid) %>% sample_n(1) %>% ungroup() label_browser_static(data_star) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: > Forschungsfragen: Wie wirkt sich die Klassengröße auf die Lese-, Mathematikleistung und Partizipation aus? Gibt differentielle Effekte nach SES? ## Missingness Patters ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(naniar) vis_miss(data_star, sort_miss = TRUE, cluster = TRUE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## (Co-)Variation und Verteilung der Daten ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(GGally) ggpairs( data_star %>% select(starts_with("g4"), g3freelunch, g3classsize) %>% mutate(across(c(starts_with("g4pt"), g3freelunch), as.factor)) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`stat_boxplot()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`stat_boxplot()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_g_gally_count()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 227 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`stat_boxplot()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 14 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`stat_boxplot()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_bin()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_density()`). ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=2100} ::: ::: ## Missing Mechanisms ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) library(ggmice) ggplot(data_star, aes(x = g3tmathss, y = g4ptimpt)) + geom_miss_point(jitter = 0.1) + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png){width=2100} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(data_star, aes(x = g3tmathss, y = g3freelunch)) + geom_miss_point(jitter = 0.1) + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Multiple-Imputation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-2.png){width=2100} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} mcar_test(data_star %>% select(g4ptimpt, g3tmathss, g3freelunch, g3classsize)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 1 × 4 statistic df p.value missing.patterns 1 23.9 14 0.0471 8 ``` ::: ::: ## Imputation ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(mice) data_star_unlabeled <- data_star %>% sjlabelled::remove_all_labels() %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(across(c(g3classtype, g3freelunch), as.factor)) data_star_imp <- mice( data_star_unlabeled %>% select(-g3tchid, -classID), m = 5, seed = 1895 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` iter imp variable 1 1 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 1 2 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 1 3 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 1 4 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 1 5 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 2 1 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 2 2 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 2 3 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 3 2 g3tmathss g3treadss g3freelunch g3classsize g3classtype g4ptimpt g4ptcrit g4ptcrts 3 3 g3tmathss g3treadss
